description: Registry of StackShift protocols. Each entry has a tier that drives install mode selection and is materialized to /docs/protocol/<file or dir> during bootstrap. Entries may be a single file (`file`) or a directory (`dir`) — directories are copied recursively.

tiers.required: Workflow cannot function correctly without it — violations cause build errors, runtime errors, or schema load failures.
tiers.recommended: Quality and UX — no errors if omitted, but Sanity Studio or author experience suffers noticeably.
tiers.optional: Opt-in systems that introduce their own architecture, dependencies, or directory structure. Only install if the project adopts the system.

protocols.title: Factory Function Pattern
protocols.summary: Required shape for every new field factory. Plain objects, no defineField() wrappers. Wrong shape breaks hideInVariants at runtime.

protocols.title: Sub-Field Visibility
protocols.summary: Hiding sub-fields within a shared object without duplicate field names. Duplicates cause Sanity to reject the schema on load.

protocols.title: Variant Router (index.tsx)
protocols.summary: Rules for the section index.tsx. Missing props interface export breaks the variant file's TypeScript compile.

protocols.title: One-Time Custom Schema Setup
protocols.summary: Project-level setup to enable custom local variants. Without it, custom sections never appear in Studio. Run once per project.

protocols.title: Field Reuse First
protocols.summary: Check local and base-package field factories before creating any new field. Duplication works but pollutes the schema.

protocols.title: Hide If Variant
protocols.summary: Use hideIfVariantIn() to exclude fields from specific variants. Authors otherwise see irrelevant fields — no error, just bad UX.

protocols.title: Preview Conventions
protocols.summary: Preview blocks for arrays and objects. Image vs icon fallback decision tree. Missing previews don't error — Studio just looks empty.

protocols.title: Array Layout Conventions
protocols.summary: Grid / tags / collapsible layout options for different array content types. Author UX only.

protocols.title: Section Directory Layout
protocols.summary: initialValue/ and images/ directories for placeholder content and variant thumbnails. No errors if omitted — just poor author onboarding.

protocols.title: Accessibility
protocols.summary: WCAG 2.1 AA enforcement. When installed, UI Forge generates variants with semantic HTML, accessible names, focus visibility, and reduced-motion handling.

protocols.title: Brand
protocols.summary: Registers a project brand document so UI Forge applies voice, palette, typography, and imagery rules during variant generation.

protocols.title: Paired-Mode Contract
protocols.summary: Canonical handshake doc between StackShift and UI Forge — skill-root resolution, marker fields, flag refusal matrix, modifier composition, contract version dance.

protocols.title: Claude Design Handoff
protocols.summary: Allows Step 4 variants to be generated from a Claude Design handoff URL via UI Forge's --handoff flag and the round-trip workflow. Requires UI Forge ≥ 0.1.9.

protocols.title: Auto-Verify Hook (PostToolUse)
protocols.summary: Installs a Claude Code PostToolUse hook that runs UI Forge's verify.js on every variant write. Requires UI Forge ≥ 0.1.9.

protocols.title: Modal & Sheet
protocols.summary: Standalone modal documents linked via conditionalLink. Editors pick a modal ref; clicking opens a @stackshift-ui/sheet or @stackshift-ui/dialog overlay instead of navigating. One-time setup + per-variant workflow with UI Forge delegation.
